name: Real: Auto-refine loop after user feedback
prompt:
A user is iterating on an image. Write the TypeScript code that implements this workflow:

'I already generated an image of a cyberpunk character, but the user said the background is too cluttered and the lighting is too harsh. I need to refine the existing image. The previous generation gave me a thoughtSignature. Use it to create a refined version with a cleaner background and softer lighting. If thoughtSignature is missing, fall back to treating the previous render as just another reference image.'

Your code should export a function `refineCharacter(prevImageBase64: string, thoughtSig: string | undefined, userFeedback: string): Promise<string>` that calls Gemini and returns the refined image base64. Do NOT write a main() runner.

assertions.name: degradation_fallback
assertions.check: has fallback when thoughtSignature is missing

assertions.name: refinement_prompt
assertions.check: includes user feedback in the refinement instruction
